action_line: webfetch("\"url\":\"https://support.hubstaff.com/supported-operating-systems-browsers-devices/\",\"timeout\":30")
response:
"<webfetch-citation source_id="fpPN3rf412GntRSZLMZgl" />

The following are the supported operating systems, browsers, and devices for Hubstaff.

---

## Minimum system requirements

In accordance with the latest industry standards, we are discontinuing support for the TLS 1.0 and 1.1 encryption protocols. Some older operating systems either lack support for, or require updates to use, the newer TLS 1.2/1.3 protocols.

The latest version of our desktop client (1.6.2 and up) provides support for TLS 1.2/1.3 encryption on all supported operating system versions.

| Operating System (OS) | Requirements |
| --- | --- |
| Windows | Windows 8 or newer. Supports Windows 7 machines with TLS 1.2 enabled. To enable this on your machine, please click [here](https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/update-to-enable-tls-1-1-and-tls-1-2-as-default-secure-protocols-in-winhttp-in-windows-c4bd73d2-31d7-761e-0178-11268bb10392). IE 11 must be [updated to the latest version](https://suppo...[truncated 344 chars]...acos-catalina/) to learn more. |
| Linux | Ubuntu 10.04 LTS (Lucid Lynx) or newer. Must support TLS 1.2. |
| Chrome Extension | Google Chrome 46 or newer |
| iOS App | Version 16 or newer |
| Android App | Supports Android 5 (Lollipop or SDK 21) or newer. |

> [!info] Info
> Surface RT devices are not supported. Hubstaff works only on Windows, Linux, macOS, or ChromeOS desktops/laptops, and on iOS or Android mobile devices.  
> Hubstaff does not support devices that run on non-Apple silicon ARM processors (e.g., Snapdragon, etc.)

---

## Client installation guides

- [Mac desktop](/install-and-sign-in-into-mac-desktop-client/)
- [Linux desktop](/install-and-sign-in-into-linux-desktop-client/)
- [Chrome extension](/installing-hubstaff-chrome-extension/)
- [Mobile app](/install-hubstaff-android-device/)
- [Web timer](/web-timer/)

---

## Supported browsers

The following browsers support URL tracking:

- Chrome
- Firefox
- Microsoft Edge
- Island
- Safari"

With several project management tools integrated with Hubstaff, it’s easy to pull in project data and assign time to it. ## Smarter tracking from your Chrome Browser\\n\\nRecord the exact time spent working on different projects and tasks with the Chrome time tracker extension. Forgot to start the...[truncated 26376 chars]...nNotifications tell you when screenshots are taken and when you start and stop the timer. So, there’s no question about when and how Hubstaff is working. ## What each app tracks\\n\\nSee how the Hubstaff apps differ in what they capture and keep track of while you’re working.\\n\\ntable_image-2x-how-tracking-works-1.png ### Customizable for each person\\n\\nYou can set preferences in Hubstaff for each individual. While we’re showcasing all functionality here, your organization might choose to turn off or customize certain features. ## Desktop permissions\\n\\nFollow our guiding principles of transparency, access, and control with customizable permissions for every member of your team.\\n\\ndesktop-permissions-table-image-2.png PRINCIPLE 3\\n\\n## Access to all your own information\\n\\nYou always can access your own data and see exactly what management sees. Further, Hubstaff’s default settings allow you to delete your time and the information attached to it whenever you choose.\"]}]}"

action_line: websearch("\"objective\":\"Find Brave official Chromebook install instructions page\",\"search_queries\":[\"Brave Chromebook install official\",\"Brave browser Chromebook install\",\"Brave Linux Chromebook instructions\"],\"mode\":\"basic\"")
response: "{\"session_id\":\"NyB144YOmMMxPuTV\",\"results\":[{\"source_id\":\"uDIRbsbK-xxVQnZRcRh_U\",\"url\":\"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MuOEGPUYeTA\",\"title\":\"How to Install the Brave Browser on a Chromebook - YouTube\",\"publish_date\":null,\"excerpts\":[\"Jul 3, 2023 · In this video, I show you how to install the brave browser on a Chromebook.\"]},{\"source_id\":\"cd9Mch7E6jTInlq7kOWPL\",\"url\":\"https://community.brave.app/t/installing-brave-browser-on-chrome-os-with-linux/483352\",\"title\":\"Installing Brave Browser on Chrome OS with Linux\",\"publish_date\":null,\"excerpts\":[\"Apr 20, 2023 · HI , Complete newbie here. Just got a new Asus Chromebook and need to install Brave Browser. I managed to get Linux set up however when i\"]},{\"source_id\":\"rA_Z4I7C1n3h-oM0cOFyI\",\"url\":\"https://windowsforum.com/threads/brave-on-chromebook-3-easy-installation-methods.386557/\",\"title\":\"Brave on Chromebook: 3 Easy Installation Methods - Windows Forum\",\"publish_date\":null,\"excer...[truncated 27296 chars]...tpak, make sure portal helpers are installed:\\n\\n```\\nsudo apt install xdg-desktop-portal xdg-desktop-portal-gtk -y\\n```\\n\\nLog out and back in after installing portal packages so your desktop session picks them up.\\n\\n## Related Brave and Ubuntu Browser Guides\\n\\nThese resources help with Brave-specific documentation and nearby Ubuntu browser choices:\\n\\n* [Brave Linux installation page](https://brave.com/linux/) for the current upstream repository commands and Snap or Flatpak notes.\\n* [Brave release notes](https://brave.com/latest/) for current stable, beta, and nightly desktop releases.\\n* [Brave privacy features](https://brave.com/privacy-features/) for Brave Shields, Tor private windows, sync privacy, and VPN details.\\n* [Install Google Chrome on Ubuntu](https://linuxcapable.com/install-google-chrome-on-ubuntu-linux/) if you need Google’s official Chromium-based browser and Google account integration.\\n* [Install Tor Browser on Ubuntu](https://linuxcapable.\"]}]}"

action_line: webfetch("\"url\":\"https://brave.com/linux/\",\"timeout\":30")
response:
"<webfetch-citation source_id="sB7HiEf3IU3A2AQT7K4CC" />

## Install with one command

```bash
curl -fsS https://dl.brave.com/install.sh | sh
```

[View source](https://dl.brave.com/install.sh) Verify signature
```bash
curl -fsSLO "https://dl.brave.com/install.sh{,.asc}" && gpg --keyserver hkps://keys.openpgp.org --recv-keys D16166072CACDF2C9429CBF11BF41E37D039F691 && gpg --verify install.sh.asc
```

Brave is supported on 64-bit AMD/Intel (amd64 / x86\_64) and ARM (arm64 / aarch64) architectures. See our full [system requirements](https://support.brave.app/hc/en-us/articles/360021357112-What-are-the-system-requirements-to-install-Brave) for minimum OS versions.

The current signing keys are also available from [https://brave.com/signing-keys/](/signing-keys/).

---

## Release Channel Installation

### Debian, Ubuntu, Mint

```bash
sudo apt install curl

sudo curl -fsSLo /usr/share/keyrings/brave-browser-archive-keyring.gpg https://brave-browser-apt-relea...[truncated 2147 chars]...ing the Snap.

## GitHub

(Pre-)releases for all channels are also [available on GitHub](https://github.com/brave/brave-browser/releases) in DEB/RPM/ZIP formats ([changelog](https://github.com/brave/brave-browser/blob/master/CHANGELOG_DESKTOP.md)).

## Unofficial packages

These packages are community maintained, and therefore we take no responsibility for them.

### Manjaro

```bash
sudo pacman -Sy brave-browser
```

### Solus

```bash
sudo eopkg update-repo
sudo eopkg install brave
```

The Solus package is a repackaging of the.deb file in to the Solus software format (.eopkg). It is currently maintained by Jacalz.

### FreeBSD

See [https://github.com/mrclksr/linux-browser-installer](https://github.com/mrclksr/linux-browser-installer) for a script to install the official Brave packages for Ubuntu inside of a chroot on FreeBSD. Note that this chroot is running an [unsupported version of Ubuntu](https://github.com/mrclksr/linux-browser-installer/issues/59)."

HI, Complete newbie here. Just got a new Asus Chromebook and need to install Brave Browser. I managed to get Linux set up however when i navigate to the Brave Browser instructions for installing on Linux it means nothing to me. Can anyone give me a real basic version of how I get this browser onto my chrome book. Thanks

At bare minimum, for folks to offer any useful guidance: what Linux version have you installed?

A good resource for some Linux versions:

Hi Thanks for taking the time to reply. Here is what I think are the version details.

PRETTY\_NAME=“Debian GNU/Linux 11 (bullseye)”  
NAME=“Debian GNU/Linux”  
VERSION\_ID=“11”

ID=debian  
HOME\_URL=“ [https://www.debian.org/](https://www.debian.org/) ”  
SUPPORT\_URL=“ [https://www.debian.org/support](https://www.debian.org/support) ”  
BUG\_REPORT\_URL=“ [https://bugs.debian.org/](https://bugs.debian.org/) ”

Hello [@FlashGMJ](/u/flashgmj)

you can install brave from the playstore or do you want to install the desktop version of brave on chromebook?

and have a nice day

[@FlashGMJ](/u/flashgmj) Here is your answer! [https://www.linuxmadesimple.info/2020/03/how-to-install-brave-browser-on.html](https://www.linuxmadesimple.info/2020/03/how-to-install-brave-browser-on.html)

Here is another great answer … maybe even better since it comes from Brave directly: [https://brave.com/linux/](https://brave.com/linux/)"

"<webfetch-citation source_id="ys5wX2yAD6vH1A6CCv-wD" />

### AI assistance

Supercharge your development workflow with Gemini integrated directly into Chrome DevTools. Streamline debugging with AI assistance for styling, performance, network and sources.

[Try it now](https://chrome.dev/devtools-ai-quickstart/#intro)

## Run first prompts

Get the feeling of Gemini in Chrome DevTools by running the example prompts in the interactive tutorial below.

### Fix styling bugs

Styling issues can be difficult to debug. Get a detailed explanation of your element's styles and get help in fixing layout and styling bugs:

```
Can you center this element?
```

### Analyze network requests

Request and response headers often contain important information not obvious at a glance. Use AI assistance to dig in:

```
Why does this request fail?
```

### Understand source files

It's rare you wrote all the code on your website - not sure what a certain script is used for?...[truncated 3245 chars]... of the supported locations.

[Check out the requirements](/docs/devtools/ai-assistance/get-started#requirements)

[

](/docs/devtools/settings/ai-innovations)

### Opt-in

AI assistance is disabled by default. To enable, go the Settings > AI assistance section. You need to agree to Google Terms of Service to be able to use AI assistance.

[Enable AI innovations](/docs/devtools/settings/ai-innovations)

[

](/docs/devtools/ai-assistance/get-started#data-use)

### Data

An Enterprise policy can be used to control whether or not the data shared with Gemini is logged and used for training

[Learn how your data is used](/docs/devtools/ai-assistance/get-started#data-use)

## Help us get better!

This feature is experimental and subject to future changes. It may generate inaccurate or offensive information that doesn't represent Google's views. Vote on the responses to help us improve it and keep the feedback coming!

[Share feedback](https://crbug.com/364805393)"

action_line: webfetch("\"url\":\"https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/ai-assistance/performance/#data-privacy\",\"timeout\":30")
response:
"<webfetch-citation source_id="fnsjzauWtMXDpsVWgaoSy" />

DevTools provides an **AI assistance** panel that helps you understand your website and fix issues by chatting with a built-in AI agent.

The **AI assistance** panel is powered by Gemini and does the following:

- Specializes in web development.
- Can answer general questions about the whole web page as well as provide specific insights about a variety of topics, including but not limited to styling, networking, performance, and more.
- Autonomously narrows down and selects specific context for you to chat about, such as DOM elements, network requests, performance trace events, and more.
- Can perform autonomous actions such as run audits and record performance traces.
- Provides a step-by-step walkthrough of its actions and reasoning and links to relevant parts of DevTools, so you can inspect them with a click.
- Can suggest code changes and generate a prompt with its insights for *your* coding agent to execute.
\...[truncated 7097 chars]...ous interactions between you and the AI.

AI assistance saves your conversation history between sessions, so you can access your previous chats even after DevTools or Chrome reload.

Use the controls in the top-left corner of the panel to control your conversation history.

#### Start new

To start a new conversation with the currently selected conversation context, click the button.

#### Continue

To continue a past conversation, click the button and select it from the context menu.

#### Delete

To delete a conversation from history, click the button.

## Rate answers and provide feedback

**AI assistance** is an experimental feature. Therefore we are actively looking for your feedback to learn how we can improve answer quality and the overall experience.

### Vote answers

Rate an answer using the **Thumbs up** and **Thumbs down** buttons below the answer.

### Report answers

To report inappropriate content, click the button next to the voting buttons."

"<webfetch-citation source_id="wy4Sl3cWPlig4lD3s_rnR" />

DevTools provides an **AI assistance** panel that helps you understand your website and fix issues by chatting with a built-in AI agent.

The **AI assistance** panel is powered by Gemini and does the following:

- Specializes in web development.
- Can answer general questions about the whole web page as well as provide specific insights about a variety of topics, including but not limited to styling, networking, performance, and more.
- Autonomously narrows down and selects specific context for you to chat about, such as DOM elements, network requests, performance trace events, and more.
- Can perform autonomous actions such as run audits and record performance traces.
- Provides a step-by-step walkthrough of its actions and reasoning and links to relevant parts of DevTools, so you can inspect them with a click.
- Can suggest code changes and generate a prompt with its insights for *your* coding agent to execute.
\...[truncated 7097 chars]...ous interactions between you and the AI.

Use the controls in the top-left corner of the panel to control your conversation history.

#### Start new

#### Continue

## Rate answers and provide feedback

action_line: webfetch("\"url\":\"https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/ai-assistance/get-started\",\"timeout\":30")
response:
"<webfetch-citation source_id="WQCOfwwJ6bT7U_yi58f-o" />

Matthias Rohmer

Use the **AI assistance** panel to learn more about how your website works with the help of AI.

## Overview

The **AI assistance panel** lets you chat with Gemini directly in DevTools. Conversations you start from this panel automatically have context about technical details of the page you are inspecting.

When using the **AI assistance** panel you can either use provided example prompts or your own questions as a starting point for conversations and continue with as many follow-up questions as needed to solve your task.

Chats in the **AI assistance** panel can help you to understand more about:

- [**Styling**](/docs/devtools/ai-assistance/styling): Ask about elements from the [DOM tree](/docs/devtools/dom) and learn why they are displayed a certain way, how they interact with each other, and solve styling challenges with provided fixes.
- [**Network requests**](/docs/devtools/ai-assistance/...[truncated 3305 chars]...ty

In managed Chrome environments availability of **AI assistance** and [Console Insights](/docs/devtools/console/understand-messages) is controlled by the [`DevToolsGenAiSettings`](https://chromeenterprise.google/intl/en_us/policies/#DevToolsGenAiSettings) Enterprise [policy](https://support.google.com/chrome/a/answer/9037717).

Unmanaged users may use the same policy to disable AI innovations on their machine and remove `Ask AI` context menu items.

1. Stop all instances of Chrome
2. Set recommended policy
	- **macOS**: Run `$ defaults write com.google.Chrome DevToolsGenAiSettings -integer 2`
		- **Linux**: Create `policies.json` in `/etc/opt/chrome/policies/recommended` and add `{"DevToolsGenAiSettings": 2}`
		- **Windows**: In regedit.exe, navigate to `HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Google\Chrome`. Set the key `DevToolsGenAiSettings` to `dword:00000002`
3. Start Chrome and go to chrome://policy to verify `DevToolsGenAiSettings` has a Policy Value of 2."

action_line: webfetch("\"url\":\"https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/ai-assistance/data-usage-and-enterprise-controls\",\"timeout\":30")
response:
"<webfetch-citation source_id="j_OEJGPYNvDuIBV4jKi7Y" />

This guide provides an overview of the data shared with Google when you use AI innovations in Chrome DevTools and explains the options available for both enterprise administrators and individual users to control these features.

## Feature availability and configuration

The availability and data usage settings for AI assistance in DevTools vary depending on the user type. The following table summarizes the default behavior and configuration options. For more details on how to configure AI assistance see the [controlling AI features section](/docs/devtools/ai-assistance/data-usage-and-enterprise-controls?tab=t.wql146ec0iyw#heading=h.ppos6pxkpgm9).

| **Customer category** | **Default state** | **Allow feature without improving AI models** | **Disable feature** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Consumers** | Allow AI features and improve AI models. | n/a | Supported by settings |
| **Workspace Education** | Allow AI fe...[truncated 6212 chars]...e environments availability of AI assistance and [Console Insights](/docs/devtools/console/understand-messages) is controlled by the [DevToolsGenAiSettings](https://chromeenterprise.google/intl/en_us/policies/#DevToolsGenAiSettings) [Enterprise policy](https://support.google.com/chrome/a/answer/9037717).

### Unmanaged users

Unmanaged users may use the same policy to disable AI innovations on their machine and remove Ask AI context menu items.

1. Stop all instances of Chrome
2. Set recommended policy
	1. **macOS**: Run `$ defaults write com.google.Chrome DevToolsGenAiSettings -integer 2`
		2. **Linux:** Create `policies.json` in `/etc/opt/chrome/policies/recommended` and add `{"DevToolsGenAiSettings": 2}`
		3. **Windows:** In `regedit.exe`, navigate to `HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Google\Chrome`. Set the key `DevToolsGenAiSettings` to `dword:00000002`
3. Start Chrome and go to chrome://policy to verify `DevToolsGenAiSettings` has a Policy Value of `2`."

"Done. I left these pages open:

- Hubstaff support: https://support.hubstaff.com/supported-operating-systems-browsers-devices/
- Brave official install page: https://brave.com/linux/
- Brave Chromebook community thread: https://community.brave.app/t/installing-brave-browser-on-chrome-os-with-linux/483352
- Chrome DevTools AI docs: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/ai-assistance
- Chrome Guest-mode troubleshooting thread: https://support.google.com/chrome/thread/268104358/can-t-open-chrome-except-in-guest-mode?hl=en

Summary:

- **Hubstaff on Chromebook/ChromeOS:** Hubstaff’s support page says ChromeOS desktops/laptops are supported, but the install table does **not** show a normal ChromeOS desktop installer. It lists Windows, Mac, Linux, then **Chrome Extension: Google Chrome 46 or newer**, and the client guides point to Chrome extension and web timer options. <citation refs="fpPN3rf412GntRSZLMZgl">Hubstaff lists “Chrome Extension | Google Chrome 46 or newer” and includes “Chrome extension” and “Web timer” under client installation guides.</citation> So the practical Chromebook path is browser-based, not a standard desktop app install.

- **Hubstaff supported ChromeOS option:** Use the **Hubstaff Chrome timer extension**, or web timer. Hubstaff’s extension page says to install from the Chrome Web Store, search “Hubstaff,” choose “Hubstaff Time Tracker,” then “Add to Chrome.” <citation refs="hO9HfaKjcEw924CpqL_8a#4">Hubstaff’s install guidance says to visit the Chrome Web Store, search for Hubstaff, click “Hubstaff Time Tracker,” then “Add to Chrome.”</citation>

- **Brave Chromebook setup link:** Brave’s own Chromebook community thread points Chromebook users who have Linux enabled to Brave’s official Linux install page, and the official Brave page gives the current install command and Debian/Ubuntu/Mint apt steps. <citation refs="2k-22t2hHQ-oWJqz-Yk8M">The Brave community thread says the better answer “comes from Brave directly” and li...[truncated 2077 chars]..."
